content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 9, 1992

Richard Zampa
District Council of Iron Workers
of the State of California and Vicinity
825 Alfred Nobel Drive, Suite C
Hercules, CA 94547

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-358

Dear Mr. Zampa:

This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice provided to Pat Frodsham of your office on
June 16, 1992, regarding the propriety of using campaign funds to
send members of your committee to New York City as delegates to
the Democratic National Convention under the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note, the Commission
cannot advise with respect to the application of federal law, such
as the Federal Election Campaign Act.

This letter confirms that your letter dated June 17, 1992,
accurately summarizes the advice I provided to Ms. Frodsham on
June 16, 1992. Section 89513(a)(1) provides:

(a) Campaign funds shall not be used to pay
or reimburse the candidate, the elected officer, or
any individual or individuals with authority to
approve the expenditure of campaign funds held by a

________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-92-358
Page 2


committee, or employees or staff of the committee
or the elected officer's governmental agency for
travel expenses and necessary accommodations except
when these expenditures are directly related to a
political, legislative, or governmental purpose.

     (1) For the purposes of this section,
payments or reimbursements for travel and
necessary accommodations shall be considered
as directly related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose if the
payments would meet the standards similar to the
standards of the Internal Revenue Service
pursuant to Sections 162 and 274 of the
Internal Revenue Code for deductions of travel
expenses under the federal income tax law.

     Thus, the test set forth in Section 89513(a)(2) is whether
there is a direct relationship between a political, legislative,
or governmental purpose and the travel.2 As I stated in my
telephone conversation with Ms. Frodsham, sending delegates to the
Democratic National Convention appears to be directly related to a
political purpose.

     I did caution, however, that other expenditures, beyond the
travel and accommodations, would need to analyzed independently to
determine if the expenditures are directly related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose. Moreover, Internal Revenue
Code Section 162(a)(2) sets forth general rules which delineate
the ordinary and necessary expenses for travelling away from home
in the pursuit of a trade or business. Pursuant to this section,
campaign funds may only be used for "business portions" of the
trip. To the extent that any day is not a business day (such as
days on which there are no meetings or formal activities),
campaign funds may not be used.3 Finally, personal activities not
related to a governmental purpose may not be paid for with

_________________
2 Section 89513(a)(2) provides that payments or reimbursements
for travel and necessary accommodations shall be considered as
directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental
purpose if the payments would meet standards similar to the
general rules in Internal Revenue Code Sections 162 and 274. The
Commission is presently developing regulations defining when
travel is directly related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose.

3 A travel day is considered a business day if it is followed
immediately by a business day. Where a weekend falls between
business days, the weekend will be counted as business time.

Our File No. A-92-358
Page 3


campaign funds, even if those activities, such as sightseeing,
occur on a business day.

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.4

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By: John W. Wallace
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JWW:aa




4
     Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question: whether it is proper to use campaign funds to send members of your committee to New York City as delegates to the Democratic National Convention under the provisions of the Political Reform Act
sections.conclusion: Section 89513(a)(1) provides that campaign funds shall not be used to pay or reimburse travel expenses and necessary accommodations except when these expenditures are directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. Sending delegates to the Democratic National Convention appears to be directly related to a political purpose. However, other expenditures beyond travel and accommodations must be analyzed independently, and only the 'business portions' of the trip may be funded under IRS standards. Personal activities not related to a governmental purpose, such as sightseeing, may not be paid for with campaign funds even if they occur on a business day.
sections.question_synthetic: May campaign funds be used to pay for travel and accommodations for committee members attending the Democratic National Convention as delegates under California's Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Campaign funds may be used for travel and accommodations to send delegates to the Democratic National Convention as it constitutes a political purpose, but only for actual business days and business-related portions of the trip under IRS standards; personal activities and non-business days cannot be funded.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: FPPC response letter confirming prior telephone advice. Document lacks formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers but contains clear question statement and conclusion. Minor OCR artifacts present (e.g., 'jj' instead of 'not'). Document is well-structured advice letter responding to request for confirmation of prior telephone consultation.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: whether it is proper to use campaign funds to send members of your committee to New York City as delegates to the Democratic National Convention under the provisions of the Political Reform Act

CONCLUSION: Section 89513(a)(1) provides that campaign funds shall not be used to pay or reimburse travel expenses and necessary accommodations except when these expenditures are directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. Sending delegates to the Democratic National Convention appears to be directly related to a political purpose. However, other expenditures beyond travel and accommodations must be analyzed independently, and only the 'business portions' of the trip may be funded under IRS standards. Personal activities not related to a governmental purpose, such as sightseeing, may not be paid for with campaign funds even if they occur on a business day.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice that campaign funds may be used to send committee members as delegates to the Democratic National Convention under Political Reform Act Section 89513(a), provided expenditures are directly related to a political purpose and comply with IRS travel expense deduction standards, excluding personal activities and non-business days.
